articles.headline: Kane eyes Lewandowski record: Tracking Europe's major goal, assist records
articles.description: Harry Kane is chasing Robert Lewandowski's incredible Bundesliga goal tally, while Kylian Mbappé seeks Champions League history. Here's the latest in Europe's record goal and assist tallies.

images.caption: Neither Kylian Mbappé nor Lamine Yamal are likely to break Lionel Messi's single-season goal and assist marks in LaLiga.

images.name: Inter Milan's Lautaro Martinez [1296x729]
images.caption: Lautaro Martinez easily leads the Seire A scoring chart, but he won't get near the single-season record.

images.name: Mason Greenwood aug 2025 [1296x729]
images.caption: Mason Greenwood was made to share the Ligue 1 top scorer title last season.

images.name: Vinicus Junior, Kylian Mbappe [1296x729]
images.caption: Kylian Mbappé is within reach of Cristiano Ronaldo's single-season Champions League goal record.
images.credit: David S. Bustamante/Real Madrid via Getty Images

article.headline: Heidenheim bag 1st Bundesliga win on record day for Schmidt
article.description: Promoted Heidenheim beat visitors Werder Bremen 4-2 on Sunday to notch their first ever Bundesliga victory on Sunday.

images.name: 1. FC Heidenheim 1846 vs. Werder Bremen - Game Highlights
images.caption: Watch the Game Highlights from 1. FC Heidenheim 1846 vs. Werder Bremen, 09/17/2023

video.headline: 1. FC Heidenheim 1846 vs. Werder Bremen - Game Highlights
video.caption: Watch the Game Highlights from 1. FC Heidenheim 1846 vs. Werder Bremen, 09/17/2023
video.title: 1. FC Heidenheim 1846 vs. Werder Bremen - Game Highlights
video.description: Watch the Game Highlights from 1. FC Heidenheim 1846 vs. Werder Bremen, 09/17/2023

tracking.trackingName: SOC_One-Play (1. FC Heidenheim 1846 vs. Werder Bremen - Game Highlights) 2023/09/17 ESHEET

article.story:
Promoted Heidenheim beat visitors Werder Bremen 4-2 on Sunday to notch their first ever Bundesliga victory on the day that coach Frank Schmidt set a record for the longest stint at a German professional club.

Heidenheim, who on the previous match day had earned their first point with a draw against Borussia Dortmund, struck at the start and end of the first half through a fifth-minute Tim Kleindienst penalty and former Werder player Eren Dinkci in the 44th to go 2-0 up.

- Stream on ESPN+: LaLiga, Bundesliga, more (U.S.)

Werder came back with goals from Marvin Duksch and Mitchell Weiser but Heindenheim scored again with a well-placed Dinkci shot in the 68th before another former Werder player, Jan-Niklas Beste, made sure of their first Bundesliga win on Schmidt's 16th anniversary as Heidenheim head coach.

Schmidt became the longest-serving coach of a German professional club with 5,844 days in charge, having taken over the team in 2007 when Heidenheim was in the fifth tier.

Volker Finke had held the record with 5,843 days as coach of Freiburg between 1991 and 2007.

keyEvents.text: First Half begins.
Goal!  1. FC Heidenheim 1846 1, SV Werder Bremen 0. Tim Kleindienst (1. FC Heidenheim 1846) converts the penalty with a left footed shot to the bottom left corner.

Goal!  1. FC Heidenheim 1846 2, SV Werder Bremen 0. Eren Dinkçi (1. FC Heidenheim 1846) left footed shot from the centre of the box to the bottom right corner. Assisted by Tim Kleindienst   following a fast break.
First Half ends, 1. FC Heidenheim 1846 2, SV Werder Bremen 0.
Second Half begins 1. FC Heidenheim 1846 2, SV Werder Bremen 0.
Substitution, SV Werder Bremen. Olivier Deman replaces Anthony Jung.
Substitution, SV Werder Bremen. Milos Veljkovic replaces Marco Friedl.
Substitution, SV Werder Bremen. Marvin Ducksch replaces Dawid Kownacki.
Penalty saved! Marvin Ducksch (SV Werder Bremen) fails to capitalise on this great opportunity,  right footed shot saved  in the top left corner.
Goal!  1. FC Heidenheim 1846 2, SV Werder Bremen 1. Marvin Ducksch (SV Werder Bremen) header from the centre of the box to the centre of the goal    following a set piece situation.
Omar Traoré (1. FC Heidenheim 1846) is shown the yellow card for a bad foul.
Goal!  1. FC Heidenheim 1846 2, SV Werder Bremen 2. Mitchell Weiser (SV Werder Bremen) header from the centre of the box to the top left corner. Assisted by Olivier Deman with a cross.
Substitution, SV Werder Bremen. Rafael Borré replaces Nick Woltemade.

Goal!  1. FC Heidenheim 1846 3, SV Werder Bremen 2. Eren Dinkçi (1. FC Heidenheim 1846) right footed shot from the centre of the box to the bottom right corner. Assisted by Jan-Niklas Beste with a cross.
Substitution, 1. FC Heidenheim 1846. Adrian Beck replaces Norman Theuerkauf.
Substitution, 1. FC Heidenheim 1846. Denis Thomalla replaces Marvin Pieringer.

Goal!  1. FC Heidenheim 1846 4, SV Werder Bremen 2. Jan-Niklas Beste (1. FC Heidenheim 1846) right footed shot from outside the box to the bottom left corner. Assisted by Tim Kleindienst   following a fast break.
Substitution, 1. FC Heidenheim 1846. Kevin Sessa replaces Eren Dinkçi.
Substitution, 1. FC Heidenheim 1846. Florian Pick replaces Jan-Niklas Beste.
Substitution, SV Werder Bremen. Justin Njinmah replaces Christian Groß.
Substitution, 1. FC Heidenheim 1846. Thomas Keller replaces Tim Kleindienst.
Niklas Stark (SV Werder Bremen) is shown the yellow card for a bad foul.
Second Half ends, 1. FC Heidenheim 1846 4, SV Werder Bremen 2.
